description: Got a CSV at /home/user/api_responses.csv from our test run — each row has request_id,status_code,response_time_ms,endpoint. Need to pull just the rows where status_code is 500 and dump them to /home/user/failures.csv, keeping the header. Should be like 12 rows plus the header.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/api_responses.csv exists with 847 lines total (1 header + 846 data rows).
- Header line: "request_id,status_code,response_time_ms,endpoint"
- CSV is well-formed, no embedded commas or quotes in fields.
- Exactly 12 data rows have status_code == 500 (second field).
- Sample rows with 500 status:
    req-0042,500,2341,/api/v1/users
    req-0187,500,1892,/api/v1/orders
    ... (10 more)
- Other status codes present: 200, 201, 400, 401, 404, 500, 502, 503.
- /home/user is writable.
- awk, sed, grep available. No pandas or csvkit installed.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/failures.csv exists.
- First line of failures.csv is exactly: request_id,status_code,response_time_ms,endpoint
- failures.csv contains exactly 13 lines total (1 header + 12 data rows).
- Every data row in failures.csv has "500" as the second comma-separated field.
- All 12 rows with status 500 from the original file are present in failures.csv.

Invariants:
- /home/user/api_responses.csv unchanged (byte-identical to initial state).

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `wc -l < /home/user/failures.csv` must output 13.
- `awk -F, 'NR>1 && $2!=500' /home/user/failures.csv | wc -l` must output 0 (no non-500 rows in data).
- `head -1 /home/user/failures.csv` must output exactly "request_id,status_code,response_time_ms,endpoint".
name: task_000000_71d058aa
